TextENGNew: If there's one time of day at school that truly lets you breathe, it's lunchtime.
We pushed our desks together as always and laid out everyone's bentos.
changeReason: Old says 'we all ate together'; new says 'laid out everyone's bentos.' The JPN describes arranging/laying out bentos (並べる), not eating.

TextENGNew: Oh my! Mion-san, your lunchbox looks truly magnificent today, it does!
...There are so many little dishes — it looks positively splendid.

Satoko and Rika-chan cried out in admiration at Mion's bento. ...Let me see.
Oh!
I just packed up some leftovers from last night. The village elders were over drinking at my house yesterday. So I set some aside for myself.
Ahahahaha! So that's why everything in there looks like drinking snacks.

``...Hmm.@k@vS19/01/HR_KEI22910. ...They may be leftovers — scraps, even — but when arranged so neatly in a bento box, they make quite an impression!@k@vS19/01/HR_KEI22920. It's like a kaiseki box lunch from a fine restaurant!``
changeReason: Old uses generic 'full-course meal at a high-class restaurant'; new uses 'kaiseki box lunch' which matches the JPN 懐石弁当 specifically.

TextENGNew: People form most of their impressions on first contact, right? So I figured the taste of a bento must be proportional to how beautiful it looks when you open it!
You may seem like you're rough around the edges, Mion-san, but you have quite the eye for presentation.
Her room is always tidy too. Mii-chan is far more careful and considerate than everyone thinks!
...Once it's in your stomach, it's all the same!
To think that Mion, who seemed like she'd say something like that, had this kind of taste.
...Hmm. You really can't judge a book by its cover.
Mion never puts a foot wrong, no matter what she does.
...Never careless, never sloppy, never off. Right — she is our club president, after all!
...Rena's bento is nothing to look down on, either. Her crab kamaboko flower is just lovely.
You know, Rena, your bento always has this artistry to it. Like the little rabbit-shaped apple slices and the carrot flowers.

Rena's bento wasn't just about the taste... How do I put it.
There was something like heart in it.
Not just delicious — it made your chest feel warm.

Ahahaha!
Thank you.
It's not quite up to Mii-chan's level, but I did want it to be nice and cute, hau!
Cooking isn't technique — it's love. In that sense, I don't even come up to your ankles.

Love, huh?
...Yeah.
Just looking at her bento, I could feel how earnestly Rena had made it.
That's just the kind of bento it was!

So it's not just taste that matters in a bento — appearance counts too.
We'll have to take a lesson from this.

Oh. Then let's have a look at the up-and-coming bentos of Satoko and company!
Wh — oh?!
...Ta-da.
Ohh-ho-ho-ho! The neighbors gave us some meat yesterday, so today it is a grilled meat bento!
Now that's... quite something!!
Packed to bursting with hearty grilled meat!
At first glance, it was almost too much for a girl's bento — but with everyone sharing it at the table, it was more than enough to make things lively!!
Hm. ...It looks like a rough, sporty bento at first, but there are actually quite a few side dishes in here. Braised spinach, stewed hijiki...
Rika-chan is really good at this kind of simple, homestyle cooking, isn't she? ...Yep, I think Rika-chan will make a wonderful bride someday!
changeReason: Old says 'great wife'; new says 'wonderful bride.' JPN お嫁さん specifically means bride, not wife.

TextENGNew: ...My future husband is already firmly in my grasp.
...Rika-chan's husband, hm.
...On the surface, that sounds quite appealing... But something tells me their married life won't be anything like straightforward...
Still, everyone is amazing.
They're all so young, and yet every one of them can cook for themselves.
I've done a bit of cooking on family camping trips... but making daily side dishes is a level I simply can't reach.
Cooking as a leisure activity is all I really amount to.
When I think about it like that, everyone really is something.
I sincerely respect them.
...Wait.
...Something tells me this isn't just someone else's problem...?
...Come to think of it, this morning, half-asleep over breakfast... Mom was saying something really important......
Ah — aaaaahhhh!!
I gave a wild yelp and leapt to my feet, startling everyone all at once.
...W-W-What's the matter?! The matter?!
*cough cough*!! You made me choke by shouting so suddenly!!
...There are rice grains coming out of Satoko's nose.
...I'd forgotten.
...All this talk about cooking for yourself... It applied to me too...!
Keiichi. You know, your father and I need to go back to Tokyo for work again.
...Mmhm. ...Oh yeah...? *yaaawn*... Sleepy...
``Your father's mentor collapsed right before a major event.@k@vS19/00/502700006. It's a very important event, and they simply can't leave a gap.``
changeReason: Old says 'fell ill'; new says 'collapsed.' JPN 倒れちゃった means collapsed/fell down, which is significantly more severe.

TextENGNew: ...If it's Dad's work, there's nothing to do about it. ...Munch munch...
So your father and I need to rush to Tokyo to help with the event. Depending on how things go, we may be away for several days.
...That sounds hard... *yaaawn*...
If we're not there, you'll have to cook all your own meals while we're gone. Are you going to be all right, Keiichi?
...I'll be fine, I'll be fine. Humans can do anything when they're backed into a corner...
Is that so? What a relief. You can at least cook rice, right? And you can make miso soup too. Then if you just pick up a few side dishes, you'll have a proper meal.
A meal?
...Yeah. I can cook rice over a camp stove. ...Never used a rice cooker before, though. Munch munch...
...I've only ever made instant miso soup with hot water too... But I'll manage somehow. Munch munch munch...
If you can cook for yourself, you won't need much money, will you? No eating out — it's too expensive. You're at the age where you should be learning to cook and manage money, after all.
...Yeah yeah... I'll manage, I'll manage...
Like I'll manage!!
What was I thinking... half-asleep, and I agreed to something completely insane!!
Just like the manga heroes always do at a moment like this, I threw my head back and raked my hands through my hair furiously. Scratch scratch!!
Hm. Your parents really do have it rough, don't they, Keiichi-kun.
You have to look after things properly while they're away!
That's not the point — food is! My parents are gone! I have to make everything myself, you hear?! This is a complete emergency!!
Then simply resolve yourself and cook for yourself. Keiichi-san, did you not make a splendid curry at the curry party not long ago?
Uh... listen. All I can manage is the curry Mom taught me on a day trip. That's the absolute ceiling of my abilities...!
I can't eat curry three times a day!! It's basically junk food!! No nutritional balance whatsoever, honestly just completely — mgh!
Y-You idiot — shhhhhhh!!
Mion and Satoko clapped their hands over my mouth!
At that very moment, the classroom door rattled open and Chie-sensei — notorious zealot of all things curry — stuck her head in.
...Did someone just say something unkind about curry?
N-N-No, not at all! Not at all!
Every face went white and every head shook in denial.
The teacher's gaze rolled over the room — that stare of hers was nothing short of inhuman...!
``...Is that so?@k@vS19/22/501400059. ...Must have been my imagination then...``
changeReason: Old says 'must have been the wind'; new says 'must have been my imagination.' JPN 空耳 means mishearing/imagining sounds, not wind.

TextENGNew: Chie-sensei swept the room one more time with those eyes,
then closed the door and went back to the staff room...
...I may have been shouting, but for her to pick it up from the staff room clear across the hallway... Sensei is truly terrifying...
...Kei-chan. You can trash talk any other food you like... but curry is off-limits. At least as long as you're living in Hinamizawa!
...Ugh, you're right. I'm sorry...
That said... I still can't eat curry three times a day.
Cup ramen tastes good because you only have it once in a while. Eat it every meal and you'd get sick of it before long.
Rice, miso soup, and a proper side dish... That's the Japanese table. That's what it should be.
Why don't you just give cooking a try, Keiichi-kun? The food you make yourself really is something special.
For this old man, it's more of a hassle than a treat.
But a bit of experience every now and then isn't a bad thing!
Exactly! Life is all experience, wouldn't you say?
...Though cutting your fingers and burning yourself is just heartbreaking, so heartbreaking.
...With everyone cheering me on, I started to feel like maybe I could actually do it if I tried.
(Though one particular person didn't quite seem to be cheering...)
Cooking is a skill I'll need to master eventually anyway, since I dream of living on my own someday.
...It wouldn't be a bad idea to learn while I have the chance...?
...It does make me quite uneasy, but...
You're right... If I don't pick up at least a little cooking skill around here, the next time we have a cooking match at club I'll be in real trouble.
Ohh-ho-ho-ho! Indeed!
That clever trick of yours from the last curry contest won't work every time, after all!
Satoko mocked me with barely concealed contempt, ridiculing that life-or-death curry showdown from which I had somehow pulled a last-ditch victory.
And who are you to be so relaxed?! You left everything to Rika-chan during that match, didn't you?!
What exactly did you cook that time?! You dumped a whole bag of salt in Rena's curry, another bag of salt in Mion's rice, and then you flipped over my pot!!
The old grudge from that curry contest flared back up!!
For now, I vented my irrepressible fury by twisting Satoko's ear.
changeReason: Old says 'pinching Satoko's ears' (plural); new says 'twisting Satoko's ear' (singular). Different action verb and number.

TextENGNew: Ow ow ow ow ow!!
That was a club activity — my attacks were part of it!! I may not look it, but I can cook at least a little, you know!!
Oh really?! That time you couldn't even peel a single carrot and had to leave it all to Rika-chan?! Don't make me laugh!!
People improve every single day, you know?! If you think I'll always be hopeless at cooking, you are very much mistaken!! Right, Rika?!
...I worry about the pot and the gas burner when I'm not watching. Sometimes you forget to turn the gas off at the main valve too.
Wah ha ha ha!! Everyone burst out laughing.
Leaving gas on is a problem before we even get to cooking!! Even I would never do that!
I don't want to hear that from someone who lives on cup ramen!!
But, but... Satoko-chan can actually cook reasonably well, you know?
It's fine, Rena — you don't need to stick up for someone like her.

No, it's not really sticking up for her. It's just that Rika-chan's cooking is so head and shoulders above everyone else's that Satoko's gets overlooked. Satoko has at least mastered the basics, right?
...At the very least, I can cook rice and miso soup.
Weeeell, though she can't tell cauliflower and broccoli apart.
T-They both taste the same once you boil them!!
Satoko erupted with indignation, raising both fists above her head. After that, everyone laughed for a good long while.
Wah ha ha ha!! Gaaah ha ha ha ha!!
Mgaaahh!! I don't mind the others so much! But I absolutely cannot stand being laughed at by you, Keiichi-san!!
Gah ha ha ha! Yes, perhaps my cooking skill does fall short of everyone else's. But I am fully confident I would never lose to you, Satoko! To you, at least!
Wh-What did you say?! Of all the nerve — claiming you're confident... That's absolutely preposterous!!!
Appearances can be deceiving, and I take turns with Rika cooking our meals, you know?! I am perfectly capable of cooking for myself, thank you very much!!
Is that so? Good for you for being able to cook. I'll buy you some candy as a reward later.
I ruffled her head patronizingly. Ruffle ruffle ruffle.
...Satoko seemed to realize she was being mocked, because her face went crimson before my eyes and steam began to pour from her like a kettle.
Mgaaahh!! You don't believe me at all, do you?! Anyway, anyway! I am a far better cook than you, Keiichi-san!!
Oh? Then if you can tell me the difference between cauliflower and broccoli, I'll believe you. Come on — let's hear it.

``...Mmmgh... um...@k@vS19/04/500400154. The green one is broccoli...@k@vS19/04/500400155. ...or, wait, that's not it... the yellow one...@k@vS19/04/500400156. Cauliflower is... blue... red... ...green...@k@vS19/04/500400157. ...Mmmmgh...!!``
changeReason: New includes colors 'blue...red...' from Satoko's confused guessing that old version omitted. JPN text includes 青…赤… (blue...red...).

TextENGNew: Come on now. What color is broccoli? Blue, red, green — it's not a TV set! Come on, say it, say it!
...Mmmmgggghhhh...!!!
Satoko-chan can cook just fine without knowing what color bwoccoli is, wight? Good girl, good girl!!
...Waaaahhhh!! Stupid Keiichi-san!! Waaaahhhh!!
There was no need to cry that hard about it.
...She's maddening. In the most endearing way.
changeReason: Old says 'She's so god damn cute'; new says 'She's maddening. In the most endearing way.' Different framing though similar sentiment; JPN いちいち可愛いヤツめ emphasizes the 'every little thing she does' aspect the new captures better.

TextENGNew: I ruffled her head for all I was worth. Ruffle ruffle ruffle!
Hmm. If you're going to talk that big, Kei-chan, you can surely show us something better than what Satoko makes...!
Mion said this with a somewhat wicked grin.
In truth, well... I had absolutely no confidence in my cooking. But somehow the tide carried me, and I decided to strike a grand pose.
Just you wait.
Tonight I shall cook an utterly magnificent dinner! I'll make a bit extra and pack it for tomorrow's bento — when you see it, you'll fall right over!!
...That sounds delightful. I'm very much looking forward to tomorrow's lunch.
Rika-chan patted me on the head.
...Urgh, those words of "looking forward to it" sounded terribly hollow to my ears...
Ahahahaha. Rena's excited too! No matter what you make, Rena won't laugh!
In that case... I had better put my full effort into my bento tomorrow too.
...So let's do this!
How about a bento showdown tomorrow?!
As a proper club activity, of course! And it comes with a punishment game!
Oh my, what an inspired idea!! What say you, Keiichi-san? You may still withdraw while you have the chance.
Satoko wiped her tears and bared her fangs, as though this were her chance to turn the tables...!!
...Uh... ...maybe this is... bad...?
Somehow the conversation has... before I knew it, gotten wildly out of hand...
If I apologize now... is it still not too late...?
But what came out of the mouth of Keiichi Maebara was a declaration so reckless it frightened even me.
Oh yeah!! Bring it on!! My bento tomorrow will knock you flat!!
Ohhhhhh...!!!
Everyone cried out in astonishment at my bold, supremely confident reply...!
...Aaaghhh... I've gone and done it! What possessed me to say something so irresponsible?!
So, Mion-san? What sort of delightful punishment game will the loser have to endure?
Satoko smiled, fearless and cunning.
...As if to say that this delightful punishment game was already all but assigned to me.
...Ku-ku-ku... Let's see.
...How about this?
...Whoever loses tomorrow's bento showdown... at lunch break tomorrow... goes to find Chie-sensei eating in the staff room... and says bad things about curry to her face!!
M-Mii-chan!! T-That's...!!!
...How... how terrifying...!!
At the sheer horror of the punishment game... everyone's faces went pale and their jaws fell open...
...Chie-sensei — a woman who would stake her life on curry — known by many names, including Curry Great Demon... no, Curry Queen...
changeReason: New includes character nicknames 'Curry Great Demon...Curry Queen' present in JPN (カレー大魔王/カレー女王) that old version omitted entirely.

TextENGNew: To her face... say bad things about curry... ?!
What would become of us...?
Even imagining it was frightening.
As everyone stood numb at the truly dreadful punishment game... Rika-chan murmured quietly.
...Tomorrow's bento will be settled by skill.
Rena and Mion and Satoko. Everyone nodded slowly, deeply, in agreement.
Though for Keiichi-san, the more pressing issue may be tonight's dinner rather than tomorrow's bento...
Ohh-ho-ho-ho!
Hah! What a pity you won't get to witness the spectacular dinner I'm about to make tonight!! I'll let you have the merest glimpse of it in tomorrow's bento, so look forward to it!!
That is quite a declaration. ...You do understand what happens if you show up with something dreadful, don't you?!
Ku-ku-ku-ku!! I simply cannot wait to see what Chie-sensei does to you, Satoko, after tomorrow!! Ku-ku-ku-ku!!
Oh, I cannot wait either!! I can already see you, Keiichi-san, prostrating yourself before her and sobbing — please, please spare me!! Yes, I can picture it perfectly!!
Wh-What was that?!
Yank!
Mgaaaahhhhh!!!
Stretch!
Half in tears, the two of us seized each other's cheeks and pulled...!!
Stretch!
Ah-ha-ha-ha! Cornering yourself to squeeze out every last drop of desperation... Kei-chan, that is so manly!!
That is not manly in the least! It is called being reckless! And he can barely even cook!!
Stretchhhh!!
Ahahahaha. Rena believes in you! Looking forward to your wonderful bento!
...I'm very much looking forward to seeing what tomorrow's Keiichi produces.
...Their expectant gazes were painful...
...I only meant to tease Satoko a little... How did it come to this...?!

Y-Yeah...
Look forward to it...!
Ah-ha-ha-ha-ha!!
Ahhhh...! I... why do I keep opening my mouth and saying things like this?!
After talking such utter nonsense... what on earth is going to become of my dinner?!
And what about tomorrow's bento showdown?!
No — more than that... If they make me stand before Chie-sensei and say bad things about curry... what will happen to me...?!
The sweltering June afternoon dragged on, the gap between the outer me — all bluster and bravado — and the inner me — screaming — growing ever wider...
That whole day.

...All the way until school let out, the whole time.
...My meal that evening and my bento the next day were the only things I could think about.
...And now that I thought about it...
...Mom is truly remarkable for planning three meals a day, every day of the year.
...I understand now just how ungrateful I've been for complaining whenever the same dish showed up two days in a row...

Aaagghh... But more importantly... what am I going to do about tomorrow's punishment game...
And before that, what am I even going to eat tonight...?
Oh, woe...
Keiichi-kun? Keiichi-kun...?

...Hm?
Oh, sorry.
I was just... lost in thought...

About what to make tonight?
Or about tomorrow's bento showdown?

...Urk.
...She'd hit the mark exactly, and the words wouldn't come.
And I wore an expression that made it perfectly obvious she'd hit the mark.
I think for tomorrow's showdown, you'll be fine as long as you do your best making your bento. There's nothing superior to a handmade bento that you put everything into.
...Tomorrow's showdown is one thing, but tonight's dinner is what worries me... I might just keel over from food poisoning...
I couldn't keep up the bravado any longer...
I let my shoulders sag dramatically and heaved a sigh from the very pit of my stomach.
Rena burst into laughter at the sight of me.

You really had no confidence in your cooking at all, did you, Keiichi-kun?
Ahahahaha.
So she'd seen right through me... urgh...
If it looks like you absolutely can't manage, no matter how hard you try, you can always call Rena.
Eh... R-Really...?!
But only if you've genuinely tried your best first, okay? You mustn't rely on others without putting up a struggle yourself.

Rena came across as easygoing, but she could be surprisingly instructive at times.
She didn't just coddle you without condition.
At moments like this, she seemed a little older somehow.

...If I absolutely can't manage, I'll call you.
...Which, frankly, seems rather likely.
You can do it! You grandstanded so magnificently at Satoko-chan that you've simply got to try your best now!
Hey — how good is Satoko, really... at cooking...?

Ahahahaha.
Better than you, at the very least.

So blunt.
...That one hit where it hurt.

You were teasing her quite a lot, after all.
...Maybe you deserve to suffer just a tiny bit, hmm? *snicker*

...Urrgghh...
Rena, you really are... surprisingly cruel...

Rena isn't all sweetness, all sweetness!
Ah-hahahahahaha!
Ahhh... After provoking Satoko that much, I couldn't possibly show up with some pathetic excuse for a bento...
Ahhhh... What is wrong with my mouth...!

All right, see you. Good luck.
With the cooking!
Show everyone that you can do it when you put your mind to it!
Y-Yeah...
I gave a feeble wave in reply.
...Leaving me with words of encouragement and a few pieces of very basic cooking advice, Rena disappeared beyond the stand of trees...
